channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Return your 2024 holiday gifts by these deadlines
item.description: To lessen the pressure a little bit, major brands, including Best Buy, Macy's, and Walmart, have extended their return policies.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: New York City taxi cab plows into group of pedestrians on Christmas Day, leaves at least 6 injured
item.description: At least six people are injured after a taxi cab slammed into a group of individuals at a busy intersection in New York City on Christmas Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NFL's Netflix debut on Christmas Day kicks off without a glitch
item.description: Patrick Mahomes and Travis Kelce exposed a glitch in Pittsburgh's defense during Netflix’s NFL debut on Christmas Day. The broadcast itself went off just fine, quickly becoming the second-most popular live title on Netflix to date, according to NFL Media.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 people slashed inside Grand Central Terminal during fight: sources
item.description: MIDTOWN, Manhattan (PIX11) – Two people were slashed inside Grand Central Terminal on Christmas Eve, according to the NYPD. It happened around 10:15 p.m. near the Nos. 4, 5 and 6 lines. A 26-year-old woman was slashed in her neck and a 42-year-old man was slashed in the wrist, police said.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, NewsNation

item.title: Pope calls for nations to overcome divisions in Christmas address as communities celebrate worldwide
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis in his traditional Christmas message on Wednesday urged “all people of all nations” to find courage during this Holy Year “to silence the sounds of arms and overcome divisions” plaguing the world, from the Middle East to Ukraine, Africa to Asia.
item.dc:creator: SILVIA STELLACCI and COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Blake Lively's claims that movie co-star launched smear campaign gets support in publicist's suit
item.description: Blake Lively's claims that “It Ends With Us” director and co-star Justin Baldoni unleashed a smear campaign against her have gained support from a new lawsuit that Baldoni's former publicist filed against him.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NewsNation became the voice for all Americans in 2024
item.description: Through town halls and in-depth reporting, NewsNation met communities where they are across the country.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Mississippi authorities searching for escaped convicted killer
item.description: GREENE COUNTY, Miss, (WJTV) – The Mississippi Department of Corrections (MDOC) is searching for a convicted killer who escaped from a Greene County prison Tuesday afternoon. The MDOC inmate, 33-year-old Drew Johnson, is a white male with blond hair and blue eyes.
item.dc:creator: Garret Grove, NewsNation

item.title: Azerbaijani airliner crashes in Kazakhstan, killing 38 with 29 survivors, officials say
item.description: An Azerbaijani airliner with 67 people onboard crashed Wednesday near the Kazakhstani city of Aktau, killing 38 people and leaving 29 survivors, a Kazakh official said.
item.dc:creator: KATIE MARIE DAVIES and DASHA LITVINOVA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Hawaii crime boss dies of overdose in federal custody: medical examiner
item.description: Michael Miske died of "toxicity of fentanyl and para-fluorofentanyl," the medical examiner’s office said in a statement obtained by AP. The death appears to have been accidental.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Airliner carrying 67 people crashes in Kazakhstan
item.description: An Azerbaijani airliner with 67 people onboard crashed Wednesday near the Kazakhstani city of Aktau, leaving at least 32 survivors, according to officials. More than 30 people may be dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Jackpot for Mega Millions over $1 billion after no numbers match on Christmas Eve drawing
item.description: Hang onto those stocking-stuffer lottery tickets. The Mega Millions jackpot has surpassed $1 billion after no ticket matched all six numbers drawn on Christmas Eve. That means the estimated jackpot for Friday's drawing is $1.15 billion, potentially the fifth-largest jackpot in the game's history.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Pope Francis kicks off a yearlong Jubilee that will test his stamina and Rome's patience
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis kicked off the 2025 Holy Year on Tuesday, inaugurating a celebration of the Catholic Church that is expected to draw some 32 million pilgrims to Rome in a test of the pope's stamina and the ability of the Eternal City to welcome them.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: China and Japan agree to talks on security issues as they seek to mend ties
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China and Japan agreed Wednesday to set up talks on often contentious security issues as they seek to improve a relationship riven in recent years by a range of issues, from territorial disputes to the discharge of water from Japan's tsunami-wrecked nuclear power plant.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Odd Oregon laws that may surprise you, such as one that prohibits predicting the future
item.description: Oregon has several strange laws still technically enforceable. Laws involving fortune-telling and playing golf in parks are among the state's oddest.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US census data shows how Middle Eastern and Hispanic residents differ in racial identity
item.description: As the U.S. Census Bureau gets public feedback about how it should tally people into new race and ethnicity groups, the agency has released new research reflecting how U.S. residents from different backgrounds regard their racial and ethnic identities. Earlier this year, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Medical examiner says a Hawaii crime boss died of a drug overdose while in federal detention
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — A Hawaii crime boss who died in federal detention this month was killed by an opioid overdose, Honolulu’s medical examiner said Tuesday. Michael Miske, 50, died of “toxicity of fentanyl and para-fluorofentanyl,” the medical examiner’s office said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Richard Perry, record producer behind 'You're So Vain' and other hits, dies at 82
item.description: Richard Perry, a hitmaking record producer with a flair for both standards and contemporary sounds whose many successes included Carly Simon’s “You’re So Vain,” Rod Stewart’s “The Great American Songbook” series and a Ringo Starr album featuring all four Beatles, died Tuesday. He was 82.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mexico to open 25 shelters for deported migrants
item.description: Baja California Gov. Marina del Pilar Ávila Olmeda announced the Mexican government will open 25 new shelters to house deported migrants.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: Stunning photos show lava erupting from Hawaii's Kilauea volcano
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — One of the world's most active volcanoes spewed lava into the air for a second straight day on Tuesday. The eruption of Kilauea volcano on Hawaii's Big Island has stayed within the mountain's summit caldera inside Hawaii Volcanoes National Park. No homes were threatened.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blake Lively allegations mirror Amber Heard case: Lawyer
item.description: Amber Heard condemned an alleged smear campaign against Blake Lively, who accused her "It Ends With Us" director of on-set sexual harassment.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Teen cheerleader allegedly murdered by ex-boyfriend days before Christmas, family says
item.description: A family in California is mourning the death of 17-year-old cheerleader Angelina Camillia Gonzales who was violently murdered just days before Christmas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas, Florida, Arizona and Idaho likely to gain House seats after 2030 census
item.description: Texas, Florida, Arizona and Idaho are likely to gain House seats after the 2030 census, according to consulting firm Election Data Services.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Danny DeVito recalls spilling Coke on floor while attending Blazers game in 1975
item.description: Beloved Hollywood celebrity Danny DeVito appeared on former NFL wide receiver Julian Edelman’s podcast “Games with Names” on Dec. 3, where he shared a story about a night out in Portland with Jack Nicholson while the actors were filming “One Flew Over the Cuckoo’s Nest” in January of 1975.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, NewsNation

item.title: Where is Santa? How to track his Christmas 2024 journey
item.description: Santa Claus, St. Nicholas, Babbo Natale, Père Noël — whatever you call him, the man clad in red will be here soon.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Judge rules Arkansas law allowing criminal charges against librarians is unconstitutional
item.description: FAYETTEVILLE, Ark. (AP) — A federal judge on Monday struck down key parts of an Arkansas law that would have allowed criminal charges against librarians and booksellers for providing “harmful” materials to minors. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Victim's father angered at 'despicable' timing of death sentence commutation
item.description: A Michigan man said he doesn't disagree with President Joe Biden's decision to commute the death sentence of his daughter's killer, but he called the timing "despicable.
item.dc:creator: Ken Kolker, KRON4

item.title: Inside the state and federal charges against the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare’s CEO
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A triptych of criminal charges paints a searing, sometimes disparate portrait of the man accused of ambushing and killing UnitedHealthcare’s CEO Brian Thompson as the executive arrived at a Manhattan hotel for his company’s annual investor conference.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden gives life in prison to 37 of 40 federal death row inmates before Trump can resume executions
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday announced that he is commuting the sentences of 37 of the 40 people on federal death row, converting their punishments to life imprisonment just weeks before President-elect Donald Trump, an outspoken proponent of expanding capital punishment, takes of
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Shohei Ohtani wins 3rd AP Male Athlete of the Year award, tying Michael Jordan for 1 shy of record
item.description: The Japanese superstar caps 2024 by winning The Associated Press Male Athlete of the Year for the third time, tying him with basketball great Michael Jordan. He trails only four-time winners Lance Armstrong, Tiger Woods and LeBron James.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Luigi Mangione's lawyer blasts New York officials for 'staged' perp walk
item.description: The attorney for Luigi Mangione, who has been accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, slammed New York City officials for what she referred to as a “staged” perp walk for her client last week.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, ABC 4

item.title: Ethics releases Matt Gaetz report: 5 takeaways
item.description: The report — which spans 42 pages and includes several more in exhibits — found “substantial evidence” that Gaetz violated House rules and state and federal laws.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Adnan Syed's lawyers seek to reduce his prison sentence
item.description: ANNAPOLIS, Md. (AP) — Attorneys for Adnan Syed, whose complex legal case was chronicled in the hit podcast “Serial," are seeking to have his prison sentence reduced as he awaits further court decisions.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ethics releases Matt Gaetz report: 5 takeaways
item.description: The report — which spans 42 pages and includes several more in exhibits — found “substantial evidence” that Gaetz violated House rules, state and federal laws, outlining allegations of prostitution, statutory rape and illicit drug use, among other accusations.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Closures, Social Security checks, furloughs: What a government shutdown might mean
item.description: Congress has until midnight Friday to come up with a way to fund the government or federal agencies will shut down, meaning hundreds of thousands of federal employees could be sent home — or stay on the job without pay — just ahead of the holidays.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, ABC 4
